goal: Build a stylish esports-themed Steam hot games stats app with frontend and backend in F:\openClaw\SteamGood

code-agent.task: Implement full-stack app in F:\openClaw\SteamGood using Node.js backend plus stylish frontend. Backend aggregates Steam hot games, default filter last 30 days review >= Very Positive, support tag filtering, include header images. Frontend has esports-fashion look and filter UI.
code-agent.output: Implemented full-stack in F:\openClaw\SteamGood: Express backend (/api/games/hot,/api/tags,/api/admin/rebuild-cache) with live-first+fallback Steam data strategy and cache; stylish esports frontend with review threshold + tag filtering + sorting + store header images; project runnable via npm scripts.

test-agent.task: Add automated tests for backend filtering and API response shape. Execute tests and summarize pass/fail.
test-agent.output: Added automated tests in backend/server.test.js and executed npm test successfully (3/3 pass): health endpoint, default review threshold behavior, and tag filtering correctness.

docs-agent.task: Write README.md with setup steps, architecture decisions, API contract, and why pure frontend is insufficient due to CORS and key safety.
docs-agent.output: Created README.md covering feature scope, architecture split, API contracts, run/test commands, and explicit rationale that pure-frontend is insufficient due to CORS and API key safety concerns.

monitor-bot.task: Review code quality, architecture risks, and release readiness. Provide final checklist and next steps.
monitor-bot.output: Quality review complete: architecture meets requirement (frontend/backend split), default filter >= Very Positive implemented, tag filter and header images implemented, tests passing. Risks: live Steam endpoints may throttle/fail, so fallback dataset is retained; duplicate scaffold folders from earlier bootstrap exist but do not block runtime. Next steps: normalize monorepo structure, add Redis cache and E2E UI tests.
